data.description:
## Why

While implementing the plan-review gate (epic 5fd2-a7c2-0aec-48fa) we reuse three load-bearing
subsystems — the HMAC signing/attestation surface (`rebar.signing`), the LLM workflow runtime
(`rebar.llm.runner` / `rebar.llm.workflow` executor), and the prompt/contract model
(`rebar.llm.prompts`). These are documented at the FRAMEWORK / USAGE / EVENT level
(`docs/llm-framework.md`, `docs/event-schema.md`, `docs/workflow-authoring-v2.md`) but there is
no single developer-facing API REFERENCE describing the reuse surface itself — the exact
function signatures, return shapes, invariants, and extension seams — for the next agent that
needs to build ON this machinery (as the plan-review gate does). This ticket creates that
reference, for both human and LLM audiences.

## What / Scope

A new doc (or docs) that FULLY describe, as a reuse/extension reference:

1. **The signing surface (`src/rebar/signing.py`)** — `sign_manifest`, `verify_signature`,
   `verify_record`, `signing_key`, `key_fingerprint`, `compute_signature`, `parse_manifest`,
   `head_sha`; the manifest format, the HMAC-SHA256 + PAYLOAD_VERSION canonicalisation, the
   environment-key custody model (`REBAR_SIGNING_KEY` / `.signing-key`), the
   certified|mismatch|foreign_key|unsigned verdict vocabulary, the SIGNATURE event +
   last-writer-wins replay, and the head_sha staleness binding. Document the close-gate
   consumption pattern AND how a NEW gate (e.g. plan-review at claim) reuses it.

2. **The LLM workflow runtime (`rebar.llm.runner`, `rebar.llm.workflow` executor)** — the
   `Runner` protocol (`run`/`preflight`), `RunRequest` (every field incl. `mode`,
   `execution_mode`, `output_schema`, `extra_tools`), `get_runner`, the PydanticAI runner, the
   structured-output stack (`structured.py`), and `run_workflow` (the declarative-IR executor:
   inputs/run_id/dry_run, RunResult, step kinds). Cover BOTH single-call operations (like
   `verify_completion`) AND declarative workflows — even parts not used by plan review.

3. **The prompt/contract model (`rebar.llm.prompts`)** — `get_prompt`, `resolve_prompt`, the
   front-matter closed-key contract (inputs/outputs schema-names, execution_mode, category),
   `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md` override resolution, the derived prompt index + CI drift gate, and
   how to add a new reviewer/prompt with a structured output contract.

4. **The output-schema/contract seam (`rebar.llm.findings`, packaged JSON Schemas)** — how an
   operation declares its own `output_schema`, where schemas live, validation
   (`validate_structured`), citation resolution, and finding normalization.

Audience: both human developers and LLM agents. Cross-link from `docs/llm-framework.md` and
`CLAUDE.md`. This is documentation only — no behavior changes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A developer-facing API reference exists (new doc under `docs/`, e.g. `docs/reuse-surface.md`,
      or focused additions to existing docs) covering the signing surface, the LLM workflow
      RUNTIME api (runner + workflow executor), the prompt/contract model, and the output-schema
      seam — each with exact function signatures and return shapes.
- [ ] The signing section documents the manifest format, HMAC/PAYLOAD_VERSION canonicalisation,
      the environment-key custody model, the verdict vocabulary
      (certified|mismatch|foreign_key|unsigned), the head_sha staleness binding, and a worked
      example of a NEW gate reusing `sign_manifest`/`verify_signature` (not just the close gate).
- [ ] The LLM-runtime section documents the `Runner` protocol, every `RunRequest` field, the
      structured-output contract path, and `run_workflow` declarative execution — including parts
      not used by the plan-review epic.
- [ ] The prompt/contract section documents `get_prompt`/`resolve_prompt`, the front-matter
      closed-key contract, `.rebar/prompts/` override resolution, and adding a new reviewer/prompt.
- [ ] Written for BOTH human and LLM audiences (concrete signatures + copy-pasteable examples).
- [ ] Cross-linked from `docs/llm-framework.md` and listed in `CLAUDE.md`'s docs pointers.
- [ ] Each documented signature/return shape is verified against the current source (no drift).
data.title: Document reusable machinery API reference: signing surface, LLM workflow runtime, prompt/contract model
